texto: People who seek psychotherapy for psychological, behavioral or relationship problems tend to experience a wide range of bodily complaints...The body can express emotional issues a person may have difficulty processing consciously...I believe that the vast majority of people don't recognize what their bodies are really telling them. The way I see it, our emotions are music and our bodies are instruments that play the discordant tunes. But if we don't know how to read music, we just think the instrument is defective.
explicacao: Citação de Charlette Mikulka sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. “People who seek psychotherapy for psychological, behavioral or relationship problems tend to expe…” articula reflexao, emotional-intelligence, emotions em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como termino e emotions.

frase_original: People who seek psychotherapy for psychological, behavioral or relationship problems tend to experience a wide range of bodily complaints...The body can express emotional issues a person may have difficulty processing consciously...I believe that the vast majority of people don't recognize what their bodies are really telling them. The way I see it, our emotions are music and our bodies are instruments that play the discordant tunes. But if we don't know how to read music, we just think the instrument is defective.

texto: How we perceive, feel about and respond to people and situations is far more guided by the lessons of early childhood than we would like to believe. We may be adults, chronologically and physically, but too often the youngest parts of our personality are invisibly, yet actively, living our lives.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Charlette Mikulka: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de sentido da existência e experiência vivida. O trecho “How we perceive, feel about and respond to people and situations is far more guided by the lesson…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que vida, healing, psychology pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de psychology e relationships.

frase_original: How we perceive, feel about and respond to people and situations is far more guided by the lessons of early childhood than we would like to believe. We may be adults, chronologically and physically, but too often the youngest parts of our personality are invisibly, yet actively, living our lives.

texto: One of the most astounding dynamics in human relationships is how the unconscious intuition of our brain's right hemisphere is able to act as radar to find us just the right person to provoke and recreate our childhood attachment relationship ...I've given much thought to the question of why we are designed to be drawn -like a moth to a flame- to the very person who is most likely to resurrect all our childhood anguish. It seems like a cruel hoax to play on two wounded souls.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Charlette Mikulka: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a familia.
